articles.title: USS Ford has seen war, fire and plumbing woes as it nears a record long deployment
articles.description: The Ford's crew left Norfolk, Va., on June 24, initially bound for the Mediterranean. More than nine months later, the crew is now in the Red Sea for the war with Iran with no clear return date.

articles.title: Khamenei's killing renews questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
articles.description: Technology allowed the U.S. and Israel to kill Iran's Supreme Leader, but raised longstanding questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be assassinating foreign leaders.

articles.title: Joe Kent, a top counterterrorism official, resigns citing Iran war

analysis.summary: The US-Iran war has escalated with Israel killing senior Iranian officials including security chief Ali Larijani, while the US struck Iranian missile sites near the Strait of Hormuz with bunker buster bombs. Trump sought international coalition support to secure the Strait of Hormuz but was rebuffed by European allies, prompting him to claim the US doesn't need help. National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent resigned in protest of the war, and Iran retaliated with missile strikes killing two in Israel while debris from interceptions rained on Dubai.

Trump publicly stated the US does not need help to secure the Strait of Hormuz
US forces struck Iranian missile sites near the Strait of Hormuz
Iran launched retaliatory missile strikes that killed two people in Israel
The USS Ford carrier is deployed in the war zone
The war has caused rising oil and gas prices
Ali Larijani was among the most senior Iranian leaders still alive after earlier strikes killed top officials

stories.headline: Joe Kent Resigns as National Counterterrorism Center Director Over Iran War

articles.title: Top US counterterrorism official resigns over Iran war, urging Trump to 'reverse course'
articles.description: National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent says Trump "started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby".

articles.title: Joe Kent, a top counterterrorism official, resigns citing Iran war
articles.description: Kent said he "cannot in good conscience" back the Iran war. In his resignation letter, he says Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation."

articles.title: In His Resignation Letter, Joe Kent Spoke About the Death of His Wife
articles.description: Chief Petty Officer Shannon Kent was killed in action during a special operations mission in Syria in 2019. Mr. Kent said he could not support “sending the next generation off to fight and die” in Iran.

articles.title: National Counterterrorism Center director resigns over Iran war

articles.title: Joe Kent resignation over Iran war reveals an internal MAGA Trump division
articles.description: Joe Kent’s resignation from the National Counterterrorism Center over objections to the U.S. war in Iran is putting a spotlight on divisions in the GOP and President Trump’s MAGA coalition. Until Kent’s resignation on Tuesday, criticism of Trump’s foreign policy moves and strikes in Iran had largely come from the usual GOP congressional gadflies, or…

analysis.summary: Joe Kent, director of the National Counterterrorism Center, resigned from the Trump administration over the U.S. war in Iran, stating he could not support the conflict in good conscience. In his resignation letter, Kent argued that Iran posed no imminent threat to the United States, making him the highest-level official to leave the administration over the war.

analysis.summary: Israel killed Ali Larijani, Iran's top security official and secretary of the Supreme National Security Council, in an airstrike. Iran confirmed his death, with the IRGC-affiliated Fars news agency calling him 'martyred.' The killing comes amid an ongoing Israel-Iran conflict and broader geopolitical tensions including disputes over the Strait of Hormuz.

analysis.summary: Illinois held its 2026 primary elections on March 17, setting up a gubernatorial rematch between Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker and Republican Darren Bailey, who lost to Pritzker in 2022. Don Tracy won the Republican Senate primary for Dick Durbin's retiring seat, while Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton was leading the crowded Democratic Senate primary. Cook County Commissioner Donna Miller won the Democratic primary for Illinois's 2nd Congressional District, defeating former Rep. Jesse Jackson Jr.'s comeback bid.
agreements: Gov. JB Pritzker and Darren Bailey will face each other in a rematch in the November governor's race
Pritzker ran unopposed for the Democratic gubernatorial nomination
Bailey lost to Pritzker in the 2022 governor's race

Cook County Commissioner Donna Miller won the Democratic primary for Illinois's 2nd Congressional District
Donna Miller defeated former Rep. Jesse Jackson Jr. in that House primary
Jesse Jackson Jr. had served time in federal prison and was attempting a political comeback
Dick Durbin is retiring after holding his Senate seat for nearly 30 years
The Illinois primary was held on March 17, 2026

The decision came after CAF's appeal board ruled that Senegal forfeited the match when their players walked off the pitch over a refereeing decision.

articles.title: Cuba begins restoring power after energy grid collapses in nationwide blackout

articles.description: Rubio Calls for New Leadership in Cuba  Newsmax

analysis.summary: Cuba is experiencing a severe energy crisis marked by repeated nationwide blackouts, economic collapse, and increased pressure from the Trump administration. The U.S. has cut off Cuba's access to Venezuelan oil, deepening the crisis, while Cuba is exploring new measures such as allowing investment from Cuban nationals living abroad. U.S. officials, including Secretary of State Rubio, are calling for leadership change in Cuba amid talk of a potential economic deal.

analysis.summary: The 98th Academy Awards featured major wins for 'Sinners,' 'Hamnet,' and 'KPop Demon Hunters,' along with notable music performances and a controversial in memoriam segment.
